aesthetic_vibe: Technical Drafting Board / Blueprint. Highly functional, objective, and precise.

fonts.usage: Body text and standard UI elements
Mathematical equations, coordinates, labels, and data points

layout.sidebar: col-span-1 md:col-span-1 lg:col-span-1 border-r border-black/10 h-screen overflow-y-auto bg-[#F5F5F0] p-6 flex flex-col gap-8
layout.graph_area: col-span-1 md:col-span-3 lg:col-span-4 h-screen relative bg-white overflow-hidden
layout.spacing_scale: Generous internal padding for containers (p-6). Tight gaps for mathematical inputs (gap-2).

components.general_rules: Strict geometric minimalism. Override Shadcn default border-radius to 0 (rounded-none). No drop shadows. Use 1px hard borders.
components.buttons: Flat. Primary: pure black background, white text. Secondary: transparent background, black text, 1px solid black border. Use font-mono or sans uppercase.
components.inputs: Shadcn input with rounded-none, border-black/20, bg-white, focus-visible:ring-1 focus-visible:ring-black focus-visible:border-black. Font must be monospace for equations.
components.dropdowns: Shadcn Select with rounded-none, sharp corners. Use for Fit Type (Linear, Polynomial, etc.).
components.panels: Use simple stacked sections with a bottom border (border-b border-black/10) to separate 'Equations' and 'Points' inputs.
components.color_indicators: Use a small 12x12 pure square (rounded-none) next to the equation input to indicate its graph color.

interactions.graph_interactivity: Pan and Zoom enabled. Clicking on the coordinate plane should place a point. Hovering over a line should highlight the equation.
interactions.transitions: Fast, precise, 150ms ease-in-out for UI state changes.

testing.data_testid_rules: All interactive elements MUST have data-testid attributes (e.g., 'equation-input-0', 'add-point-btn', 'export-graph-btn', 'fit-type-select').

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Setup Fonts: Add Outfit, IBM Plex Sans, and JetBrains Mono from Google Fonts in index.html or global CSS.
2. Override tailwind.config.js to set '--radius' to '0rem' for the authentic Brutalist/Swiss technical look.

3. Use 'mafs' (yarn add mafs) or 'function-plot' for rendering the coordinate plane. 'mafs' provides a beautiful React-based graphing plane out of the box with pan/zoom. Alternatively, use standard D3 or Recharts if you prefer manual drawing.
4. Use 'mathjs' for formula evaluation and parsing. Use the 'regression' package to handle curve fitting (linear regression, polynomial interpolation) based on user-placed points.
5. Ensure the layout is a full viewport height (h-screen) with a fixed scrollable sidebar on the left and the graph occupying the entire right section.
6. Iterate through the 'graph_colors' array to assign unique colors to each new mathematical function.
7. Implement an export feature using 'html-to-image' to capture the graph container.
8. Keep the UI strictly monochrome (black/white/grey). The ONLY elements that should have color are the graph lines and their corresponding color-indicator squares in the sidebar.
